event: diagnosed with PWS by methylation polymerase chain reaction
started to receive recombinant growth hormone (rGH) treatment
methylphenidate for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder
presented with gonadotropin-releasing hormone deficiency and growth hormone deficiency
began taking glipizide and long-acting insulin (glargine)
advised to apply positive pressure ventilation
presented with dyspnea and loss of consciousness
exhibited dyspnea, oliguria (<100 mL/day), and abdominal distension
skin ulcers (1×3 cm in size on the left thigh and 0.5×1 cm in size on the right lower arm)
macular rash on his entire body
chest x-ray showed cardiomegaly and haziness in the right lung
admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU)
intubation with synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation (SIMV)
aspartate aminotransferase/alanine aminotransferase of 51 U/L/59 U/L
blood urea nitrogen/creatinine of 34.9 mg/dL/0.93 mg/dL
Chest computed tomography revealed bilateral pleural effusion and peribronchial opacity
provided normal saline and noradrenalin for hemodynamic restoration
testing for any respiratory virus was negative
Transesophageal echocardiography revealed a normal-sized left ventricle and normal systolic function
dilated RV cavity, decreased RV systolic function, and pulmonary hypertension
started a continuous infusion (3 mg/hr) of furosemide
placed in a special ICU bed with a pressure-relieving mattress and a weighing machine
positioning was changed every 2 hours for skin care
added clindamycin and dressed wounds daily
ventilator mode was changed from volume control (VC) SIMV to pressure-control (PC) SIMV
started enteral nutrition via a nasogastric tube
fluids and electrolytes were corrected daily according to body weight
delivered oxygen at a high flow rate of 50 L/min
fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2) was 0.4 via a nasal cannula
high flow rate was decreased to 20 L/min
biphasic positive airway pressure (BiPAP) was applied only during sleep
patient was able to receive rehabilitation training
weight had decreased to 133 kg (-52.7 kg)
discharged at a weight of 131.4 kg (-54.3 kg)
educated regarding drug adherence, diet, oxygen therapy with BiPAP only during sleep, and exercise training
visited the outpatient clinic every 3 months
HbA1c level has been maintained at 8%--9% with metformin (13.3 mg/kg/day) and insulin (degludec at 76 IU/day and aspart at 60 IU/day)
continuing oxygen therapy with BiPAP during sleep without congestive hepatopathy or elevation of the proBNP level
